text:
A patient's residence is wherever he or she makes his or her home. This may be his or 
her own dwelling, an apartment, a relative's home, a home for the aged, or some other 
type of institution. However, an institution may not be considered a patient's residence if 
the institution meets the requirements of §§1861(e)(1) or 1819(a)(1) of the Act.. 
Included in this group are hospitals and skilled nursing facilities, as well as most nursing 
facilities under Medicaid. (See the Medicare State
